title: Extra bits: destructuring, spread, try/catch
description: The rest of the everyday toolkit.

questions.code:
const { a, b = 10 } = { a: 1 };
console.log(a, b);
questions.explanation: Destructuring supports defaults for missing properties.
Array destructuring by position. Third element ignored.
Spread copies own enumerable props of a into a new object.
.message holds the string passed to Error().
finally runs after try/catch. It sets x to 'd' last.
Rename AND default: pull 'a' into 'renamed', with 5 when missing.
Rest in destructuring captures leftover elements as an array.
Set removes duplicates; spread converts it back to an array. Classic dedup trick.
